name: tasks
description: Task-as-obligation-list coordination. A $task_registry is a $space that authors a policy (roles, obligations, ordered policies per kind) and mints $task children. Each $task carries a snapshotted obligation list whose cursor advances as role-holders :pass; movement of the task is the lease, role-gated by :acceptable. Replaces the v0.3 $taskspace demo.

classes.description: A room that mints and coordinates $task children. Inherits $room (exits, look-with-exit-listing, announce machinery) so a registry can sit in a navigable layout — e.g. a host catalog can wire an `out` exit back to wherever the registry is mounted. Authors policy (roles, obligations, policies) through admin verbs and the kanban / admin UI. Tasks colocate with the registry in v1 (one DO per registry).

verbs.name: holds_role
verbs.source: verb :holds_role(actor_obj, role_name) rxd { /* True if the given actor is a member of the given role on this registry, or a wizard. Used to gate step actions; query-only. */ if (!actor_obj) { return false; } if (has_flag(actor_obj, "wizard")) { return true; } if (typeof(role_name) != "string") { return false; } if (!(role_name in this.roles)) { return false; } let info = this.roles[role_name]; let ow = info["owners"]; if (typeof(ow) != "list") { return false; } return actor_obj in ow; }

verbs.name: acceptable
verbs.source: verb :acceptable(thing) rxd { if (isa(thing, $actor)) { return true; } if (!isa(thing, $task)) { return false; } return thing.registry == this; }

verbs.name: enterfunc
verbs.source: verb :enterfunc(thing) rx { let has_policy = false; for kind, keys in this.policies { has_policy = true; } if (!has_policy && thing == actor) { tell(actor, "No policies configured. Ask the registry owner to seed one."); } return true; }

verbs.name: exitfunc
verbs.source: verb :exitfunc(thing) rx { return true; }

verbs.name: _on_release
verbs.source: verb :_on_release(child) rx { if (!isa(child, $task)) { return false; } if (!child:_is_complete()) { return false; } for edge in child.links { if (typeof(edge) != "map") { continue; } let parent = edge["to"]; if (!isa(parent, $task)) { continue; } if (parent == child) { continue; } if (parent.registry != this) { continue; } let next = []; let cleared = false; for c in parent.wait_for { if (typeof(c) == "map" && c["kind"] == "child_complete" && c["task"] == child) { cleared = true; } else { next = next + [c]; } } if (cleared) { parent.wait_for = next; parent.log = parent.log + [{ "ts": now(), "actor": $wiz, "outcome": "wait_cleared", "evidence": { "task": child } }]; parent.last_change = now(); } } return true; }

verbs.name: create_task
verbs.source: verb :create_task(kind, name, text, labels, source_ref) rxd { /* Create a new task on this registry. Pick a workflow by name (kind), give the task a short name and optional markdown instructions; the task carries a checklist copy of the workflow's steps and starts at the first step. */ if (typeof(kind) != "string" || !(kind in this.policies)) { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "unknown kind", value: kind }; } if (typeof(name) != "string" || name == "") { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "name required", value: name }; } if (typeof(text) != "string") { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "text must be a string", value: text }; } let t = create($task, { owner: actor, name: name, location: this }); t.registry = this; t.kind = kind; if (typeof(labels) == "list") { t.labels = labels; } else { t.labels = []; } if (source_ref != null) { t.source = source_ref; } let obs = []; for k in this.policies[kind] { obs = obs + [{ "key": k, "met": false }]; } t.obligations = obs; t.wait_for = []; t.links = []; t.log = [{ "ts": now(), "actor": actor, "outcome": "created" }]; t.terminal = false; t.created_at = now(); t.last_change = now(); if (text != "") { t:set_text(text); } this._tracked_tasks = this._tracked_tasks + [t]; observe({ type: "task_created", task: t, registry: this, name: name, kind: kind, source: source_ref, by: actor, ts: now() }); return t; }

verbs.name: listing
verbs.source: verb :listing() rxd { /* List every task this registry has minted, with name, workflow (kind), labels, current location, current step's role, blocked count, terminal/complete flags, link count, age, and last-change timestamp — one row per task. Use :detail for a single task's full state. */ let out = []; for x in this._tracked_tasks { if (!valid(x)) { continue; } if (!isa(x, $task)) { continue; } let cur = x:cursor(); let cur_role = null; if (cur != null) { cur_role = cur["role"]; } out = out + [{ "task": x, "name": x.name, "kind": x.kind, "labels": x.labels, "location": location(x), "cursor_role": cur_role, "wait_for_count": length(x.wait_for), "terminal": x.terminal, "complete": this:_task_complete(x), "link_count": length(x.links), "age_ms": now() - x.created_at, "last_change": x.last_change }]; } return out; }

verbs.name: _task_complete
verbs.source: verb :_task_complete(t) rxd { for o in t.obligations { if (!o["met"]) { return false; } } return true; }

verbs.name: available_actions
verbs.source: verb :available_actions(t, actor_obj) rxd { /* Return the actions a given actor can take on a given task right now (claim, mark step done, hand off, put back on board, cancel, ...) along with the args each one needs. Empty if the task is final or the actor isn't authorised. */ let out = []; if (!isa(t, $task)) { return out; } if (t.terminal) { return out; } let cur = t:cursor(); if (cur == null) { return out; } let here = location(t); if (here == this) { if (this:holds_role(actor_obj, cur["role"]) && length(t.wait_for) == 0) { out = out + [{ "verb": "claim", "label": "Claim", "args": [] }]; } } else { if (here == actor_obj) { if (length(t.wait_for) == 0) { out = out + [{ "verb": "pass", "label": "Pass", "args": [{ "name": "evidence", "type": "map", "required": false }] }]; } out = out + [{ "verb": "reject", "label": "Reject", "args": [{ "name": "i", "type": "int", "required": true }, { "name": "why", "type": "str", "required": true }] }]; out = out + [{ "verb": "wait", "label": "Wait", "args": [{ "name": "cond", "type": "map", "required": true }] }]; out = out + [{ "verb": "yield", "label": "Spawn related", "args": [{ "name": "spec", "type": "map", "required": true }] }]; out = out + [{ "verb": "handoff", "label": "Hand off", "args": [{ "name": "target", "type": "obj", "required": true }] }]; out = out + [{ "verb": "release", "label": "Release", "args": [] }]; out = out + [{ "verb": "drop_terminal", "label": "Drop", "args": [{ "name": "why", "type": "str", "required": true }] }]; } } return out; }

verbs.name: set_role
verbs.source: verb :set_role(role_name, info) rxd { /* Add or update a role on this registry. A role groups people who can claim and finish the steps it owns. Pass info as a map with optional 'description' and 'owners' (a list of actor ids). Registry owner or wizard only. */ if (this.owner != actor && !has_flag(actor, "wizard")) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "only the registry owner or wizard can edit roles" }; } if (typeof(role_name) != "string" || role_name == "") { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "role name required" }; } if (typeof(info) != "map") { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "role info must be a map" }; } let before = null; if (role_name in this.roles) { before = this.roles[role_name]; } let desc = ""; if (typeof(info["description"]) == "string") { desc = info["description"]; } let owners = []; if (typeof(info["owners"]) == "list") { owners = info["owners"]; } let normalised = { "description": desc, "owners": owners }; let next = this.roles; next[role_name] = normalised; this.roles = next; observe({ type: "registry_role_changed", registry: this, name: role_name, before: before, after: normalised, actor: actor, ts: now() }); return normalised; }

verbs.name: remove_role
verbs.source: verb :remove_role(role_name) rxd { /* Remove a role. Fails if any step still references it. Registry owner or wizard only. */ if (this.owner != actor && !has_flag(actor, "wizard")) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "only the registry owner or wizard can edit roles" }; } if (!(role_name in this.roles)) { return false; } for k, o in this.obligations { if (o["role"] == role_name) { raise { code: "E_CONSTRAINT", message: "role still referenced by an obligation", value: k }; } } let before = this.roles[role_name]; let next = {}; for k, v in this.roles { if (k != role_name) { next[k] = v; } } this.roles = next; observe({ type: "registry_role_changed", registry: this, name: role_name, before: before, after: null, actor: actor, ts: now() }); return true; }

verbs.name: set_obligation
verbs.source: verb :set_obligation(key, info) rxd { /* Add or update a step on this registry. A step is a named gate a task moves through; only members of the listed role can claim it and mark it done. Pass info as a map with 'role' (must already exist) and 'criterion' (the conditions of satisfaction — what 'done' looks like). Registry owner or wizard only. */ if (this.owner != actor && !has_flag(actor, "wizard")) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "only the registry owner or wizard can edit obligations" }; } if (typeof(key) != "string" || key == "") { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "obligation key required" }; } if (typeof(info) != "map" || typeof(info["role"]) != "string" || !(info["role"] in this.roles)) { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "obligation info must include a known role" }; } if (typeof(info["criterion"]) != "string") { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "obligation criterion must be a string" }; } let before = null; if (key in this.obligations) { before = this.obligations[key]; } let normalised = { "role": info["role"], "criterion": info["criterion"] }; let next = this.obligations; next[key] = normalised; this.obligations = next; observe({ type: "registry_obligation_changed", registry: this, key: key, before: before, after: normalised, actor: actor, ts: now() }); return normalised; }

verbs.name: remove_obligation
verbs.source: verb :remove_obligation(key) rxd { /* Remove a step. Fails if any workflow still references it. Existing tasks that already snapshotted this step keep their copy; an obligation_orphaned observation fires for each — including in-flight tasks the registry has minted but that have moved to an actor via :claim/:handoff. Registry owner or wizard only. */ if (this.owner != actor && !has_flag(actor, "wizard")) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "only the registry owner or wizard can edit obligations" }; } if (!(key in this.obligations)) { return false; } for kind, keys in this.policies { for k in keys { if (k == key) { raise { code: "E_CONSTRAINT", message: "obligation still referenced by a policy", value: kind }; } } } let before = this.obligations[key]; let next = {}; for k, v in this.obligations { if (k != key) { next[k] = v; } } this.obligations = next; observe({ type: "registry_obligation_changed", registry: this, key: key, before: before, after: null, actor: actor, ts: now() }); for t in this._tracked_tasks { if (!valid(t)) { continue; } if (!isa(t, $task)) { continue; } let touched = false; for o in t.obligations { if (o["key"] == key) { touched = true; } } if (touched) { observe({ type: "obligation_orphaned", task: t, obligation_key: key, ts: now() }); } } return true; }

verbs.name: set_policy
verbs.source: verb :set_policy(kind, keys) rxd { /* Add or update a workflow on this registry. A workflow is the ordered list of steps a task moves through; tasks pick a workflow by name when they're created. 'kind' is the workflow's name (e.g. 'bug'); 'keys' is the list of step names in order. Registry owner or wizard only. */ if (this.owner != actor && !has_flag(actor, "wizard")) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "only the registry owner or wizard can edit policies" }; } if (typeof(kind) != "string" || kind == "") { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "policy kind required" }; } if (typeof(keys) != "list") { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "policy must be a list of obligation keys" }; } for k in keys { if (typeof(k) != "string" || !(k in this.obligations)) { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "unknown obligation key in policy", value: k }; } } let before = null; if (kind in this.policies) { before = this.policies[kind]; } let next = this.policies; next[kind] = keys; this.policies = next; observe({ type: "registry_policy_changed", registry: this, kind: kind, before: before, after: keys, actor: actor, ts: now() }); return keys; }

verbs.name: remove_policy
verbs.source: verb :remove_policy(kind) rxd { /* Remove a workflow. New tasks can no longer be created with this workflow name; existing tasks keep walking their snapshotted step list. Registry owner or wizard only. */ if (this.owner != actor && !has_flag(actor, "wizard")) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "only the registry owner or wizard can edit policies" }; } if (!(kind in this.policies)) { return false; } let before = this.policies[kind]; let next = {}; for k, v in this.policies { if (k != kind) { next[k] = v; } } this.policies = next; observe({ type: "registry_policy_changed", registry: this, kind: kind, before: before, after: null, actor: actor, ts: now() }); return true; }

verbs.name: seed_minimal_policy
verbs.source: verb :seed_minimal_policy(seed_owner) rxd { if (this.owner != actor && !has_flag(actor, "wizard")) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "only the registry owner or wizard can seed" }; } let populated = false; for k, v in this.roles { populated = true; } for k, v in this.obligations { populated = true; } for k, v in this.policies { populated = true; } if (populated) { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "registry already populated; refuse to overwrite" }; } let owner_actor = seed_owner; if (!owner_actor) { owner_actor = actor; } this:set_role("doer", { description: "Does the work", owners: [owner_actor] }); this:set_obligation("do:it", { role: "doer", criterion: "Done." }); this:set_policy("task", ["do:it"]); return true; }

classes.description: A work item minted by a $task_registry. Carries a snapshotted obligation list whose cursor advances as role-holders :pass. Movement is the lease: claim/handoff/release/drop_terminal each call moveto with a transition_intent set, which is what the :acceptable gate checks. Generic take/give/drop on a $task fail because they don't set the intent.

verbs.name: cursor
verbs.source: verb :cursor() rxd { let reg_obs = this.registry.obligations; for o in this.obligations { if (o["met"]) { continue; } let key = o["key"]; if (!(key in reg_obs)) { continue; } let info = reg_obs[key]; return { "key": key, "role": info["role"], "criterion": info["criterion"] }; } return null; }

verbs.name: detail
verbs.source: verb :detail() rxd { /* Return the full state of a single task: id, name, instructions, workflow (kind), labels, ordered steps with met/role/criterion, log of who did what when, blocked-on conditions, links to related tasks, current location, and cursor. Use the registry's :listing for a per-row summary across all tasks. */ let reg_obs = this.registry.obligations; let obs = []; for o in this.obligations { let key = o["key"]; let role = null; let criterion = null; if (key in reg_obs) { let info = reg_obs[key]; role = info["role"]; criterion = info["criterion"]; } let entry = { "key": key, "met": o["met"] == true, "role": role, "criterion": criterion }; if ("evidence" in o) { entry["evidence"] = o["evidence"]; } obs = obs + [entry]; } let complete = true; for o in this.obligations { if (!o["met"]) { complete = false; } } return { "id": this, "name": this.name, "text": this.text, "kind": this.kind, "labels": this.labels, "obligations": obs, "log": this.log, "wait_for": this.wait_for, "links": this.links, "terminal": this.terminal, "complete": complete, "cursor": this:cursor(), "location": location(this), "registry": this.registry, "created_at": this.created_at, "last_change": this.last_change }; }

verbs.name: moveto
verbs.source: verb :moveto(target) rx { if (!this.transition_intent) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "$task moves require a lifecycle verb (claim/handoff/release/drop_terminal)", value: target }; } if (target != this.registry) { if (!isa(target, $actor)) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "$task can only move to actors or its registry", value: target }; } let cur = this:cursor(); if (cur == null) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "task has no live cursor" }; } if (!this.registry:holds_role(target, cur["role"])) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "target does not hold cursor role", value: cur["role"] }; } } return moveto(this, target); }

verbs.name: _authorized_moveto
verbs.source: verb :_authorized_moveto(target, intent_kind) rx { this.transition_intent = intent_kind; let err = null; try { moveto(this, target); } except e { err = e; } this.transition_intent = null; if (err != null) { raise err; } return target; }

verbs.name: claim
verbs.source: verb :claim() rxd { /* Take responsibility for delivering this task. It moves into your inventory; the current step is waiting on you. */ if (this.terminal) { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "task is terminal" }; } if (location(this) != this.registry) { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "task is not at its registry", value: location(this) }; } let cur = this:cursor(); if (cur == null) { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "task has no live cursor" }; } if (length(this.wait_for) > 0) { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "task has unresolved wait_for" }; } if (!this.registry:holds_role(actor, cur["role"])) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "actor does not hold cursor role", value: cur["role"] }; } this:_authorized_moveto(actor, "claim"); this.log = this.log + [{ "ts": now(), "actor": actor, "outcome": "claimed" }]; this.last_change = now(); observe({ type: "task_claimed", task: this, actor: actor, ts: now() }); return this; }

verbs.name: handoff
verbs.source: verb :handoff(target) rxd { /* Hand the task to another person in the same role. The current step doesn't change; only who's working on it does. */ if (location(this) != actor) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "only the holder can handoff" }; } if (!isa(target, $actor)) { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "handoff target must be an actor", value: target }; } let cur = this:cursor(); if (cur == null) { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "task has no live cursor" }; } if (!this.registry:holds_role(target, cur["role"])) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "target does not hold cursor role", value: cur["role"] }; } let from = location(this); this:_authorized_moveto(target, "handoff"); this.log = this.log + [{ "ts": now(), "actor": actor, "outcome": "claimed" }]; this.last_change = now(); observe({ type: "task_moved", task: this, from: from, to: target, actor: actor, ts: now() }); return this; }

verbs.name: release
verbs.source: verb :release() rxd { /* Put the task back on the board. Anyone in the current step's role can take it next. */ if (location(this) != actor && !has_flag(actor, "wizard")) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "only the holder or a wizard can release" }; } this:_authorized_moveto(this.registry, "release"); this.log = this.log + [{ "ts": now(), "actor": actor, "outcome": "released" }]; this.last_change = now(); observe({ type: "task_released", task: this, actor: actor, ts: now() }); this.registry:_on_release(this); return this; }

verbs.name: pass
verbs.source: verb :pass(evidence) rxd { /* Record the current step as done. The task advances to the next step, or returns to the board as Done if this was the last step. */ if (location(this) != actor) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "only the holder can pass" }; } let cur = this:cursor(); if (cur == null) { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "no live cursor (orphan or complete)" }; } if (length(this.wait_for) > 0) { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "task has unresolved wait_for" }; } let next = []; let advanced = false; for o in this.obligations { if (!advanced && !o["met"] && o["key"] == cur["key"]) { next = next + [{ "key": o["key"], "met": true, "evidence": evidence }]; advanced = true; } else { next = next + [o]; } } this.obligations = next; this.log = this.log + [{ "ts": now(), "actor": actor, "outcome": "passed", "obligation_key": cur["key"], "evidence": evidence }]; this.last_change = now(); observe({ type: "task_passed", task: this, obligation_key: cur["key"], actor: actor, evidence: evidence, ts: now() }); if (this:_is_complete()) { this:_authorized_moveto(this.registry, "release"); this.log = this.log + [{ "ts": now(), "actor": actor, "outcome": "released" }]; this.last_change = now(); observe({ type: "task_released", task: this, actor: actor, ts: now() }); this.registry:_on_release(this); } return this; }

verbs.name: _is_complete
verbs.source: verb :_is_complete() rxd { for o in this.obligations { if (!o["met"]) { return false; } } return true; }

verbs.name: reject
verbs.source: verb :reject(i, why) rxd { /* Pick an earlier step that was marked done and send the task back to it. Once that step is done again, the task continues to the next pending step. */ if (location(this) != actor) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "only the holder can reject" }; } if (typeof(i) != "number" || i < 1 || i > length(this.obligations)) { raise { code: "E_RANGE", message: "obligation index out of range", value: i }; } let target = this.obligations[i]; if (!target["met"]) { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "obligation is not met; nothing to rewind", value: i }; } let next = []; let j = 1; for o in this.obligations { if (j == i) { next = next + [{ "key": o["key"], "met": false }]; } else { next = next + [o]; } j = j + 1; } this.obligations = next; this.log = this.log + [{ "ts": now(), "actor": actor, "outcome": "rejected", "obligation_key": target["key"], "why": why }]; this.last_change = now(); observe({ type: "task_rejected", task: this, obligation_key: target["key"], actor: actor, why: why, ts: now() }); return this; }

verbs.name: wait
verbs.source: verb :wait(cond) rxd { /* Record a condition this task is waiting on. It won't progress until the condition is met. */ if (location(this) != actor) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "only the holder can wait" }; } if (typeof(cond) != "map" || typeof(cond["kind"]) != "string") { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "wait condition must be a map with a kind", value: cond }; } this.wait_for = this.wait_for + [cond]; this.log = this.log + [{ "ts": now(), "actor": actor, "outcome": "waited" }]; this.last_change = now(); observe({ type: "task_waited", task: this, actor: actor, condition: cond, ts: now() }); return this; }

verbs.name: yield
verbs.source: verb :yield(spec) rxd { /* Add a new task linked to this one. Optionally make it blocking, so this task waits for the new one to finish. */ if (location(this) != actor) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "only the holder can yield" }; } if (typeof(spec) != "map") { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "yield spec must be a map", value: spec }; } let kind = null; if ("kind" in spec) { kind = spec["kind"]; } let sname = null; if ("name" in spec) { sname = spec["name"]; } let stext = ""; if ("text" in spec && typeof(spec["text"]) == "string") { stext = spec["text"]; } let labels = []; if ("labels" in spec && typeof(spec["labels"]) == "list") { labels = spec["labels"]; } let blocking = false; if ("blocking" in spec) { blocking = spec["blocking"] == true; } let because = null; if ("because" in spec) { because = spec["because"]; } let child = this.registry:create_task(kind, sname, stext, labels, null); child.links = child.links + [{ "to": this, "role": "parent" }]; this.links = this.links + [{ "to": child, "role": "parent" }]; if (typeof(because) == "string" && because != "") { child.log = [{ "ts": now(), "actor": actor, "outcome": "created", "because": because }]; } if (blocking) { this.wait_for = this.wait_for + [{ "kind": "child_complete", "task": child }]; } this.log = this.log + [{ "ts": now(), "actor": actor, "outcome": "yielded", "link_ctx": { "to": child, "role": "parent" } }]; this.last_change = now(); observe({ type: "task_yielded", task: this, actor: actor, child: child, blocking: blocking, because: because, ts: now() }); return { "child": child, "link_role": "parent" }; }

verbs.name: drop_terminal
verbs.source: verb :drop_terminal(why) rxd { /* Mark the task final — it won't progress further. It moves to the Dropped column. */ if (location(this) != actor && !has_flag(actor, "wizard")) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "only the holder or a wizard can drop" }; } this.terminal = true; if (location(this) != this.registry) { this:_authorized_moveto(this.registry, "terminal"); } this.log = this.log + [{ "ts": now(), "actor": actor, "outcome": "dropped", "why": why }]; this.last_change = now(); observe({ type: "task_dropped", task: this, why: why, actor: actor, ts: now() }); observe({ type: "task_returned_home", task: this, reason: "drop_terminal", ts: now() }); return this; }

verbs.name: set_name
verbs.source: verb :set_name(name) rxd { if (typeof(name) != "string" || name == "") { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "set_name requires a non-empty string", value: name }; } if (!this:is_writable_by(actor)) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "cannot rename task", value: this }; } this.name = name; this.last_change = now(); observe({ type: "task_renamed", task: this, name: name, actor: actor, ts: now() }); return this; }

verbs.name: set_labels
verbs.source: verb :set_labels(labels) rxd { if (typeof(labels) != "list") { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "set_labels requires a list of strings", value: labels }; } for l in labels { if (typeof(l) != "string") { raise { code: "E_INVARG", message: "set_labels: every label must be a string", value: l }; } } if (!this:is_writable_by(actor)) { raise { code: "E_PERM", message: "cannot relabel task", value: this }; } this.labels = labels; this.last_change = now(); observe({ type: "task_relabeled", task: this, labels: labels, actor: actor, ts: now() }); return this; }

seed_hooks.name: Taskboard
seed_hooks.description: A registry that coordinates work items through a role-gated obligation cursor. Operators populate roles, obligations, and policies via the admin verbs (or :seed_minimal_policy for tests).
